# AMN2 Plugin Development Guide (llms.txt)

> This document is designed for AI coding assistants (Claude, Copilot, etc.) to autonomously build plugins for the Another-Mirai-Native2 (AMN2) QQ bot framework. It contains everything needed: project setup, API reference, patterns, and pitfalls.

## 1. Framework Overview

- TARGET: Windows only, ClassLibrary, .NET Framework 4.8 OR .NET 9.0 (choose one)
- SDK NuGet: `Another-Mirai-Native.Abstractions` (netstandard2.0)
- Native API docs: The NuGet package ships with complete XML documentation at `lib\netstandard2.0\Another-Mirai-Native.Abstractions.xml`. All public types, methods, and parameters have doc comments — refer to this file for precise signatures and descriptions not covered here.
- Message format: CQ codes. Use `MessageBuilder` (never hand-write CQ codes unless for Text passthrough).
- Plugin isolation: Plugins run in separate processes, communicating with the host via RPC. This is transparent to plugin code.
- Output naming: The SDK auto-renames output to `Native_<AssemblyName>.dll` + generates `Native_<AssemblyName>.json` manifest. These two files are your distributable.

## 2. Project Setup (MANDATORY steps)

### 2.1 .csproj requirements

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0-windows</TargetFramework>  <!-- or net48 -->
    <CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>true</CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>
</PropertyGroup>
```

- Windows-specific TFMs (net9.0-windows, net48) are required if using WinForms/WPF menus.
- `CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>true` is MANDATORY for any plugin — without it, dependencies are not copied and the plugin fails to load.
- The SDK auto-generates manifest and merges dependencies. Default behavior is correct; only customize `GenerateAmnManifest` / `CleanAmnOutputDir` / `IgnoreDependencyVersion` if needed.

### 2.2 Install the SDK NuGet

```
Install-Package Another-Mirai-Native.Abstractions
```

### 2.3 File path conventions (THIS IS CRITICAL)

The framework allocates a dedicated directory for each plugin. All plugin-generated files (config, database, cache, etc.) MUST be stored in this directory.

```csharp
string appDir = API.AppApi.GetAppDirectory();  // e.g., "D:\AMN2\data\plugins\com.example.myplugin\"
```

**Do NOT** write files to current working directory (`Directory.GetCurrentDirectory()`), program base directory (`AppDomain.CurrentDomain.BaseDirectory`), or hard-coded relative paths. Always use `GetAppDirectory()`.

When sending local images or audio, the file MUST exist under the framework's media directories:

| Media type | Required directory | Subdirectories allowed | Example path |
|------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------|
| Image | `<framework root>\data\image\` | Yes | `data\image\avatars\user123.png` |
| Audio | `<framework root>\data\record\` | Yes | `data\record\voices\greeting.amr` |

When calling `MessageBuilder.Image()` or `MessageBuilder.Record()`, use paths relative to these directories:

```csharp
// Image is at D:\AMN2\data\image\avatars\reward.png
new MessageBuilder().Image("avatars/reward.png")  // relative to data\image

// Audio is at D:\AMN2\data\record\sounds\ding.amr
new MessageBuilder().Record("sounds/ding.amr")    // relative to data\record
```

Absolute paths are also accepted but NOT recommended — plugins should use relative paths within the designated media directories.

**The file MUST already exist on disk before calling send.** The framework does not auto-upload or auto-generate images; it only references existing files.

## 3. Plugin Entry Point

### 3.1 Minimal plugin skeleton

```csharp
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Models;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Attributes;

namespace MyPlugin;

[PluginInfo(
    appId: "com.example.myplugin",   // REVERSE DOMAIN FORMAT REQUIRED
    name: "My Plugin",
    version: "1.0.0",
    description: "Does something useful",  // optional
    author: "Your Name"                     // optional
)]
public class Entry : PluginBase
{
    public override async Task OnEnableAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        API.Logger.Info("MyPlugin", "Plugin enabled!");
    }

    public override async Task OnDisableAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        API.Logger.Info("MyPlugin", "Plugin disabled, cleaning up...");
    }
}
```

### 3.2 Key rules

- `[PluginInfo]` appId MUST use reverse domain format (e.g., `com.example.myplugin`).
- The entry class MUST inherit `PluginBase`.
- The entry class MUST be `public`.
- PluginInfo can be set via `[PluginInfo]` attribute OR by overriding the `PluginInfo` property. Attribute is preferred.
- `OnEnableAsync` is called after plugin is loaded; use it for initialization (load config, connect DB).
- `OnDisableAsync` is called before plugin unload; use it for cleanup.
- Rule-of-thumb: at most ONE class should inherit `PluginBase` per plugin DLL.

## 4. Handling Events

### 4.1 Interface-based event handling

Implement one or more handler interfaces. The class does NOT need to be the PluginBase class — any `public` class works.

Available handlers and their signatures:

```csharp
// Message handlers
IGroupMessageHandler    → Task<EventHandleResult> OnReceiveGroupMessageAsync(GroupMessageContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IPrivateMessageHandler  → Task<EventHandleResult> OnReceivePrivateMessageAsync(PrivateMessageContext e, CancellationToken ct)

// Group member events
IGroupMemberIncreaseHandler → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupMemberIncreaseAsync(GroupMemberIncreaseContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupMemberDecreaseHandler → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupMemberDecreaseAsync(GroupMemberDecreaseContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupMemberBannedHandler   → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupMemberBannedAsync(GroupMemberBannedContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupMemberUnbannedHandler → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupMemberUnbannedAsync(GroupMemberUnbannedContext e, CancellationToken ct)

// Admin & whole-ban events
IAdminChangeHandler        → Task<EventHandleResult> OnAdminChangeAsync(AdminChangedContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupWholeBannedHandler   → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupWholeBannedAsync(GroupWholeBannedContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupWholeUnbannedHandler → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupWholeUnbannedAsync(GroupWholeUnbannedContext e, CancellationToken ct)

// Request handlers (add friend, add group, group invite)
IFriendAddRequestHandler   → Task<EventHandleResult> OnFriendAddRequestAsync(FriendAddRequestContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupAddRequestHandler    → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupAddRequestAsync(GroupAddRequestContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupInviteRequestHandler → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupInviteRequestAsync(GroupInviteRequestContext e, CancellationToken ct)

// Other
IFriendAddedHandler      → Task<EventHandleResult> OnFriendAddedAsync(FriendAddedContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IGroupFileUploadHandler  → Task<EventHandleResult> OnGroupFileUploadedAsync(GroupFileUploadedContext e, CancellationToken ct)
IMenuHandler             → void OnMenu(MenuContext e)  // called on UI thread!
```

### 4.2 EventHandleResult

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `EventHandleResult.Block` | Stop event propagation; no further plugins receive it |
| `EventHandleResult.Pass`  | Allow event to continue to next plugins |

### 4.3 EventPriority

```csharp
[EventPriority(PluginEventType.GroupMsg, 100)]  // Higher = earlier execution
public class MyHandler : IGroupMessageHandler { ... }
```

- `PluginEventType` enum values match handler types (e.g., `GroupMsg`, `PrivateMsg`, `GroupMemberIncrease`, etc.).
- Can be declared on either PluginBase class or handler class.

### 4.4 Context object pattern

Every handler receives a context object. Example for `GroupMessageContext`:

| Member | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `e.Message` | `Message` | Parsed message object |
| `e.Message.Text` | `string` | Raw message text |
| `e.Message.MessageChain` | `List<MessageItemBase>` | Structured message elements (At, Image, Face, etc.) |
| `e.FromQQ` | `QQ` | Sender info (`.Id`, `.NickName`) |
| `e.FromGroup` | `Group` | Source group (`.Id`, `.Name`) |
| `e.SendMessageAsync(string)` | `Task<Message>` | Quick reply to source |
| `e.Reply(string)` | `Task<Message>` | Reply with quote |
| `e.API` | `IPluginApi` | Full API access |

For request-type contexts (friend add, group add, etc.), there are additional methods:
- `e.SetRequestResultAsync(RequestHandleResult.Accept)` or `.Deny`
- `e.AppendMessage` / `e.Flag` for request data

## 5. Command Mode (alternative to raw event handling)

Inherit `CommandHandlerBase` instead of implementing message handlers manually.

```csharp
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Attributes;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Context;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Enums;

public class Commands : CommandHandlerBase
{
    [Command(MatchMode.FullMatch, "/ping")]
    public void Ping(GroupMessageContext e)
    {
        e.SendMessage("pong");
    }

    [Command(MatchMode.Regex, @"^/give (?<userId>\S+) (?<itemId>\d+) (?<count>\d+)$")]
    public Task<EventHandleResult> Give(GroupMessageContext e, string userId, int itemId, int count)
    {
        e.SendMessage($"Gave {itemId} x{count} to {userId}");
        return Task.FromResult(EventHandleResult.Block);
    }
}
```

### Match modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `MatchMode.FullMatch` | Exact match |
| `MatchMode.StartWith` | Message starts with template |
| `MatchMode.EndWith` | Message ends with template |
| `MatchMode.Contain` | Template appears anywhere |
| `MatchMode.Regex` | Regex match; named groups → method params |

### Method parameters (order-independent, all optional)

- `GroupMessageContext` — only filled for group messages; ensure Scope includes group
- `PrivateMessageContext` — only filled for private messages; ensure Scope includes private
- `CancellationToken` — cancellation token
- `string raw` — the raw message text (parameter name must be exactly `raw`)
- Named regex capture groups — auto-converted to target type via `Convert.ChangeType`

### Return types

| Return type | Behavior |
|-------------|----------|
| `void` | Treated as Block |
| `EventHandleResult` | Explicit Block/Pass |
| `Task` | Async, treated as Block |
| `Task<EventHandleResult>` | Async, explicit Block/Pass |

### DO NOT re-implement IGroupMessageHandler or IPrivateMessageHandler when using CommandHandlerBase

CommandHandlerBase already implements them. Duplicate implementation causes unpredictable dispatch.

### Override fallback

```csharp
protected override Task<EventHandleResult> OnNoMatchAsync(GroupMessageContext e, CancellationToken ct)
{
    // Called when no [Command] matches
    return base.OnNoMatchAsync(e, ct);
}
```

## 6. Sending Messages

### 6.1 Using MessageBuilder (ALWAYS use this)

```csharp
var msg = new MessageBuilder()
    .At(qq)
    .Text(" Hello!")
    .Face(CQFace.doge)
    .Image("path/to/image.png")
    .Build();

await e.SendMessageAsync(msg);
```

### Available builder methods

| Method | Parameter | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| `.Text(string)` | Text content | Raw text or CQ code string |
| `.At(long)` | QQ number | @ someone |
| `.AtAll()` | — | @ everyone (group only) |
| `.Face(CQFace)` | CQFace enum | QQ emoji; use enum (CQFace.doge, CQFace.点赞, etc.) |
| `.Image(string)` | File path | Relative to `data\image\` (file MUST exist on disk before sending) |
| `.ImageHash(string)` | 32-char hash | Reference received image by hash |
| `.Record(string)` | File path | Relative to `data\record\` (file MUST exist on disk before sending) |
| `.RecordHash(string)` | Hash | Reference received audio by hash |

### 6.2 Sending via context (preferred for event replies)

```csharp
await e.SendMessageAsync("text or MessageBuilder output");
await e.FromGroup.SendGroupMessageAsync("text");
await e.Reply("reply with quote");
```

### 6.3 Sending via API (for proactive messages)

```csharp
// In PluginBase-derived class:
await API.MessageApi.SendGroupMessageAsync(groupId, "text");
await API.MessageApi.SendPrivateMessageAsync(userId, "text");

// Message result:
var result = await API.MessageApi.SendGroupMessageAsync(groupId, "msg");
if (result.IsSuccess) { /* result.Id is message ID */ }
```

### 6.4 Deleting messages

```csharp
await API.MessageApi.DeleteMessageAsync(messageId);
// Or from Message object:
await message.DeleteMessageAsync();
```

## 7. Full API Reference

### 7.1 IPluginApi (aggregate entry point)

Access via `API` property in PluginBase, or `e.API` in context objects.

| Property | Interface | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `.Logger` | ILogger | Logging |
| `.MessageApi` | IMessageApi | Message send/delete/history |
| `.FriendApi` | IFriendApi | Friend operations |
| `.GroupApi` | IGroupApi | Group management |
| `.AppApi` | IAppApi | App info, plugin control |

### 7.2 ILogger

```csharp
API.Logger.Debug("tag", "message");
API.Logger.Info("tag", "message");
API.Logger.Warn("tag", "message");   // May show desktop notification
API.Logger.Error("tag", "message");  // May show desktop notification
API.Logger.Fatal("message");         // May show desktop notification
```

First parameter (`type`) is a free-form tag for categorizing log entries (e.g., "初始化", "处理消息").

### 7.3 IMessageApi

```csharp
// Send (returns Message with .IsSuccess, .Id)
int SendPrivateMessage(long userId, string message)
int SendGroupMessage(long groupId, string message)
// + Async variants returning Task<int>

// Delete (returns bool)
bool DeleteMessage(long messageId)
// + Async variant

// Chat history (private: groupId=0)
List<ChatHistory> GetChatHistories(long groupId, long qq, int count)
ChatHistory? GetChatHistoryById(long parentId, bool isGroup, int messageId)
// + Async variants
```

### 7.4 IGroupApi

```csharp
// Info queries
GroupInfo? GetGroupInfo(long groupId)
List<GroupInfo> GetGroupList()
GroupMemberInfo? GetGroupMemberInfo(long groupId, long qq)
List<GroupMemberInfo> GetGroupMembers(long groupId)  // slow for large groups
// + Async variants

// Moderation (requires admin/owner, can't target self or owner)
bool BanGroup(long groupId, bool enable)
bool BanMember(long groupId, long qq, long durationSeconds)  // 0 = unban
bool Kick(long groupId, long qq, bool rejectAddRequest = false)
bool Leave(long groupId)  // dissolves group if owner
// + Async variants

// Admin
bool SetAdmin(long groupId, long qq, bool isAdmin)  // owner only
bool SetMemberCard(long groupId, long qq, string card)
bool SetMemberTitle(long groupId, long qq, string title)  // owner only
// + Async variants

// Request handling
bool SetGroupAddRequest(string flag, bool accept, string refuseReason = "")
bool SetGroupInviteRequest(string flag, bool accept, string refuseReason = "")
// + Async variants
```

### 7.5 IFriendApi

```csharp
List<FriendInfo> GetFriendInfos()
bool SendPraise(long qq, int count)  // daily limits may apply
bool SetFriendAddRequest(string flag, bool accept, string card = "")
// + Async variants
```

### 7.6 IAppApi

```csharp
string GetAppDirectory()     // Plugin data directory (absolute path)
long GetLoginQQ()            // Current login QQ, 0 if not logged in
string GetLoginQQNick()      // Current login nickname
void ReloadPlugin()          // Kills the plugin process
void DisablePlugin()         // Kills the plugin process
```

Note: `ReloadPlugin()` and `DisablePlugin()` terminate the plugin process immediately — code after them will not execute.

## 8. Creating UI Windows (WinForms/WPF)

### 8.1 Prerequisites

- TFM MUST include `-windows` suffix (e.g., `net9.0-windows`). Pure `net9.0` will fail.
- Add `<UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>` or `<UseWPF>true</UseWPF>` to .csproj.

### 8.2 Menu-driven window pattern

```csharp
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Attributes;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Context;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Handlers;

[Menu("Open My Window")]
public class WindowEntry : IMenuHandler
{
    private MyForm? _form;

    public void OnMenu(MenuContext e)
    {
        if (_form == null)
        {
            _form = new MyForm();
            _form.FormClosing += (s, ev) => { _form.Hide(); ev.Cancel = true; };  // reuse
        }
        _form.Show();
    }
}
```

- `OnMenu` is called on the UI thread — safe to create/manipulate UI controls.
- Hide instead of close to reuse window instances across multiple menu clicks.
- For WPF, use `Closing` event: `e.Cancel = true; window.Hide();`.

## 9. Receiving & Parsing Messages

### 9.1 Message chain traversal

```csharp
foreach (var item in e.Message.MessageChain)
{
    switch (item)
    {
        case At at:
            Console.WriteLine($"@ {at.TargetQQ}");
            break;
        case Image img:
            Console.WriteLine($"Image: {img.FilePath} / hash:{img.Hash}");
            break;
        case Face face:
            Console.WriteLine($"Emoji ID: {face.Id}");
            break;
        case Text text:
            Console.WriteLine($"Text: {text.Content}");
            break;
        case Reply reply:
            Console.WriteLine($"Reply to msg #{reply.MessageId}");
            break;
        case RichContent rich:
            Console.WriteLine($"{rich.Type}: {rich.Content}");
            break;
        case Dice dice:
            Console.WriteLine($"Dice: {dice.Type}");
            break;
        case RPS rps:
            Console.WriteLine($"RPS: {rps.Type}");
            break;
        case Record record:
            Console.WriteLine($"Audio: {record.FilePath}");
            break;
        case Poke poke:
            Console.WriteLine($"Poke: {poke.Name}");
            break;
    }
}
```

### 9.2 CQCode model

Messages are CQ codes in format `[CQ:type,key=value,...]`. Common types:
- `[CQ:at,qq=114514]`
- `[CQ:image,file=abc123.png]`
- `[CQ:face,id=14]`
- `[CQ:record,file=audio.amr]`
- `[CQ:reply,id=123456]`
- `[CQ:dice,type=1]`
- `[CQ:json,content=...]` / `[CQ:xml,content=...]`

## 10. Build & Distribution

### 10.1 Build output

After building, find:
- `Native_<AssemblyName>.dll` — the merged plugin assembly
- `Native_<AssemblyName>.json` — auto-generated plugin manifest

These two files go into the framework's `data\plugins\` folder.

### 10.2 Native dependency notes

Some NuGet packages depend on native DLLs (e.g., `e_sqlite3.dll`, `libSkiaSharp.dll`). These are NOT auto-merged and must be placed manually in:
- The framework root directory, OR
- The `libraries\` folder

### 10.3 Framework built-in dependencies

To minimize plugin size, prefer dependencies already bundled with the framework. See `DependencyManifest-dotnet9.json` in the SDK tools folder for the list.

## 11. Error Patterns & Pitfalls

### DO NOT:
- Forget `<CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>true</CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>` — plugin silently fails to load
- Write plugin files (config, DB, cache) to CWD or base directory — use `API.AppApi.GetAppDirectory()` instead
- Send an image/audio before the file exists on disk — the framework only references existing files
- Send images from outside `data\image\` or audio from outside `data\record\` — they must be under the framework's media folders
- Implement both `CommandHandlerBase` AND `IGroupMessageHandler`/`IPrivateMessageHandler` on the same class
- Use `net9.0` TFM for UI plugins — must be `net9.0-windows`
- Throw unhandled exceptions in event handlers — they may crash the plugin process
- Call `ReloadPlugin()` or `DisablePlugin()` and expect code after to execute
- Use `GroupMessageContext` parameter in `[Command]` with `Scope = MessageScope.Private` — method won't be called; same for the reverse

### ALWAYS:
- Use `API.AppApi.GetAppDirectory()` for all plugin file storage
- Ensure images/audio files are placed under `data\image\` / `data\record\` before sending
- Use `async/await` for event handlers that call async APIs
- Check `CancellationToken` in long operations
- Return `EventHandleResult.Block` when you handled the event and want to stop propagation
- Use `MessageBuilder` instead of manual CQ code strings
- Test with the `Protocol_NoConnection` protocol for offline development

### CancellationToken usage

```csharp
public async Task<EventHandleResult> OnReceiveGroupMessageAsync(GroupMessageContext e, CancellationToken ct)
{
    if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) return EventHandleResult.Pass;
    await Task.Delay(5000, ct);  // throws OperationCanceledException if canceled
    // ... or pass ct to all async methods you call
}
```

## 12. Debugging

1. Build the plugin, copy `Native_*.dll` + `Native_*.json` to `data\plugins\`.
2. In the framework UI, click "Reload Plugins" or restart.
3. Check framework logs for your plugin name.
4. To attach a debugger: In Visual Studio, Debug > Attach to Process, find the `Another-Mirai-Native.exe` process with your plugin path in the command line.
5. Enable `DebugMode` and `DebugLazyLoad` in `conf/Config.json` to pause before `OnEnableAsync` so you can attach a debugger early.
6. Increase `LoadTimeout` and `PluginInvokeTimeout` in config when debugging to avoid timeout kills.

## 13. Complete Plugin Example

```csharp
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Attributes;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Context;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Enums;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Handlers;
using Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Models;
using System;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

namespace DicePlugin;

[PluginInfo("com.example.dice", "Dice Plugin", "1.0.0", "A dice rolling plugin", "Author")]
public class Entry : PluginBase, IGroupMessageHandler
{
    public async Task<EventHandleResult> OnReceiveGroupMessageAsync(GroupMessageContext e, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        if (e.Message.Text.Trim().ToLower() is not ("r" or "roll"))
            return EventHandleResult.Pass;

        var dice = Random.Shared.Next(1, 7);
        var msg = new MessageBuilder()
            .At(e.FromQQ.Id)
            .Text($" You rolled {dice}!")
            .Build();

        await e.SendMessageAsync(msg);
        return EventHandleResult.Block;
    }
}
```

## 14. PackageReference Checklist

When adding NuGet packages beyond the SDK, verify:
1. The package supports your target framework (net48 or net9.0-windows).
2. Any native dependencies are handled (see section 10.2).
3. The dependency is not already bundled with the framework (see section 10.3).
4. `<CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>true</CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>` is set.

## 15. Namespace Quick Reference

```
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions                    PluginBase, CommandHandlerBase
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Attributes         PluginInfo, Command, EventPriority, Menu
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Context             *Context classes (GroupMessageContext, etc.)
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Enums               CQFace, EventHandleResult, MatchMode, MessageScope, etc.
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Handlers            I*Handler interfaces
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Models              Message, MessageBuilder, Group, QQ, etc.
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Models.MessageItem  At, Face, Image, Text, Reply, etc.
Another_Mirai_Native.Abstractions.Services            IPluginApi, ILogger, IMessageApi, IGroupApi, etc.
```
